source.name: GOV.UK — Student visa: Money you need

extraSources.name: GOV.UK — Financial evidence for Student route applicants

requirements.label: CAS from a licensed student sponsor
requirements.detail: A Confirmation of Acceptance for Studies (CAS) reference from your UK university (a licensed Student sponsor).

requirements.label: Maintenance funds (held 28 days)
requirements.detail: Show living costs of £1,171/month outside London or £1,529/month inside London, for up to 9 months, held in your account for 28 consecutive days.

requirements.label: Immigration Health Surcharge (IHS)
requirements.detail: Pay the IHS as part of your application to access the NHS for the duration of your visa.

requirements.label: Approved English language proof (SELT)
requirements.detail: A Secure English Language Test (e.g. IELTS for UKVI) at the level your CAS specifies, unless exempt.

requirements.label: Valid passport
requirements.detail: A current passport or valid travel document.

requirements.label: Tuberculosis (TB) test certificate
requirements.detail: Required if you are resident in a country on the UK TB-testing list.

requirements.label: ATAS certificate
requirements.detail: An Academic Technology Approval Scheme certificate is required for certain sensitive science/technology subjects.

requirements.label: Tuition / funds transfer
requirements.detail: Transfer tuition deposits or maintenance funds with a low-FX provider.

requirements.label: Connectivity on arrival
requirements.detail: An eSIM gives you a UK number and data on landing.

requirements.label: Accommodation
requirements.detail: Arrange student housing early; book before arrival.
